contents:
(** * n-Path-split maps.

A map is n-path-split if its induced maps on the first n iterated path-spaces are split surjections.  Thus every map is 0-path-split, the 1-path-split maps are the split surjections, and so on.  It turns out that for n>1, being n-path-split is the same as being an equivalence. *)

Fixpoint PathSplit (n : nat) `(f : A -> B) : Type
  := match n with
       | 0 => Unit
       | S n => (forall a, hfiber f a) *
                forall (x y : A), PathSplit n (@ap _ _ f x y)
     end.

Definition isequiv_pathsplit (n : nat) `{f : A -> B}
: PathSplit n.+2 f -> IsEquiv f.

hypotheses.type: forall a : B, hfiber f a
forall x y : A, PathSplit n.+1 (ap f)

contents: pose (h := fun x y p => (fst (k x y) p).1).

hypotheses.type: forall a : B, hfiber f a
forall x y : A, PathSplit n.+1 (ap f)

hypotheses.body: fun (x y : A) (p : f x = f y) => (fst (k x y) p).1
hypotheses.type: forall x y : A, f x = f y -> x = y

contents:
pose (hs := fun x y => (fun p => (fst (k x y) p).2)
                         : (ap f) o (h x y) == idmap).

hypotheses.type: forall a : B, hfiber f a
forall x y : A, PathSplit n.+1 (ap f)

hypotheses.body: fun (x y : A) (p : f x = f y) => (fst (k x y) p).1
hypotheses.type: forall x y : A, f x = f y -> x = y

hypotheses.body:
fun x y : A =>
(fun p : f x = f y => (fst (k x y) p).2) : ap f o h x y == idmap
hypotheses.type: forall x y : A, ap f o h x y == idmap

contents: clearbody hs; clearbody h; clear k.

hypotheses.type: forall a : B, hfiber f a
forall x y : A, f x = f y -> x = y
forall x y : A, ap f o h x y == idmap
forall a : B, hfiber f a
forall x y : A, f x = f y -> x = y
forall x y : A, ap f o h x y == idmap
forall a : B, hfiber f a
forall x y : A, f x = f y -> x = y
forall x y : A, ap f o h x y == idmap
forall a : B, hfiber f a
forall x y : A, f x = f y -> x = y
forall x y : A, ap f o h x y == idmap
forall a : B, hfiber f a
forall x y : A, f x = f y -> x = y
forall x y : A, ap f o h x y == idmap
forall a0 : B, hfiber f a0
forall x y : A, f x = f y -> x = y
forall x y : A, ap f o h x y == idmap

goals.conclusion: transport (fun x : A => f x = b) (h a a' (p @ p'^)) p = p'

hypotheses.type: forall a0 : B, hfiber f a0
forall x y : A, f x = f y -> x = y
forall x y : A, ap f o h x y == idmap

goals.conclusion: (ap f (h a a' (p @ p'^)))^ @ p = p'

hypotheses.type: forall a0 : B, hfiber f a0
forall x y : A, f x = f y -> x = y
forall x y : A, ap f o h x y == idmap
forall a0 : B, hfiber f a0
forall x y : A, f x = f y -> x = y
forall x y : A, ap f o h x y == idmap
forall a0 : B, hfiber f a0
forall x y : A, f x = f y -> x = y
forall x y : A, ap f o h x y == idmap
forall a0 : B, hfiber f a0
forall x y : A, f x = f y -> x = y
forall x y : A, ap f o h x y == idmap
forall a0 : B, hfiber f a0
forall x y : A, f x = f y -> x = y
forall x y : A, ap f o h x y == idmap

goals.conclusion: forall (A B : Type) (f : A -> B), IsEquiv f -> Contr (PathSplit n f)

contents: simple_induction n n IHn; intros A B f ?.

hypotheses.type: forall (A0 B0 : Type) (f0 : A0 -> B0), IsEquiv f0 -> Contr (PathSplit n f0)
forall (A0 B0 : Type) (f0 : A0 -> B0), IsEquiv f0 -> Contr (PathSplit n f0)

contents:
#[export] Instance ishprop_pathsplit (n : nat) `(f : A -> B)
: IsHProp (PathSplit n.+2 f).

Definition equiv_pathsplit_isequiv (n : nat) `(f : A -> B)
: PathSplit n.+2 f <~> IsEquiv f.
(** Path-splitness transfers across commutative squares of equivalences. *)

Lemma equiv_functor_pathsplit (n : nat) {A B C D}
      (f : A -> B) (g : C -> D) (h : A <~> C) (k : B <~> D)
      (p : g o h == k o f)
: PathSplit n f <~> PathSplit n g.

goals.conclusion: PathSplit n f <~> PathSplit n g

hypotheses.type: g o h == k o f

goals.conclusion: PathSplit n f <~> PathSplit n g

hypotheses.type: g o h == k o f
g o h == k o f

goals.conclusion: PathSplit n.+1 f <~> PathSplit n.+1 g

hypotheses.type: g o h == k o f

goals.conclusion: PathSplit n.+1 f <~> PathSplit n.+1 g

hypotheses.type: g o h == k o f
g o h == k o f

goals.conclusion: PathSplit n.+2 f <~> PathSplit n.+2 g

hypotheses.type: g o h == k o f
g o h == k o f

goals.conclusion:
(forall a : B, hfiber f a) * (A -> A -> Unit) <~>
(forall a : D, hfiber g a) * (C -> C -> Unit)

hypotheses.type: g o h == k o f

goals.conclusion: (forall a : B, hfiber f a) <~> (forall a : D, hfiber g a)

hypotheses.type: g o h == k o f

goals.conclusion: hfiber f (k^-1%equiv d) <~> hfiber g d

hypotheses.type: g o h == k o f

goals.conclusion: {x : A & f x = k^-1%equiv d} <~> {x : C & g x = d}

hypotheses.type: g o h == k o f

goals.conclusion: f a = k^-1%equiv d <~> g (h a) = d

hypotheses.type: g o h == k o f

goals.conclusion: f a = k^-1%equiv d <~> k (f a) = d

hypotheses.type: g o h == k o f

goals.conclusion: PathSplit n.+2 f <~> PathSplit n.+2 g

hypotheses.type: g o h == k o f
g o h == k o f
g o h == k o f
g o h == k o f
g o h == k o f
g o h == k o f

contents: exact (isequiv_commsq f g h k (fun a => (p a)^)).

hypotheses.type: g o h == k o f

contents: exact (isequiv_commsq' f g h k p).
(** A map is oo-path-split if it is n-path-split for all n.  This is also equivalent to being an equivalence. *)

Definition ooPathSplit `(f : A -> B) : Type
  := forall n, PathSplit n f.

#[export]  Instance ishprop_oopathsplit `(f : A -> B)
: IsHProp (ooPathSplit f).

Definition equiv_oopathsplit_isequiv `(f : A -> B)
: ooPathSplit f <~> IsEquiv f.
